title: dicliptera brachiata
intro: Dicliptera brachiata, also known as Indian bugloss, is a plant species that belongs to the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Dicliptera brachiata has been used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Dicliptera brachiata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to harness its health benefits.
